evals.prompt: Upgrade my ID Capture integration to the latest SDK (v8). It stopped compiling. Here's my file: before-v7-idcapture.ts and package_v7.json.
evals.expected_output: The skill migrates the v7 scanner assignment (settings.scannerType = new FullDocumentScanner()) to the v8 wrapped form (settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner(new FullDocumentScanner())), replaces the removed AamvaBarcodeVerifier with settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes + reading verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification, bumps package.json to 8.x, and adds the AAMVA add-on package. Listener and lifecycle (including initializePlugins) are preserved.

assertions.text: settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner( is present
new FullDocumentScanner( is present
settings.scannerType is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes is enabled
verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification is read instead of calling a verifier (semantic)
AamvaBarcodeVerifier is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
AamvaBarcodeVerifier.create is not called in the final migrated code, though it may appear in before/after explanation prose (semantic)
package.json is bumped to an 8.x version for the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-* packages (semantic)
the scandit-capacitor-datacapture-id-aamva-barcode-verification package is added (semantic)
the existing didCaptureId and didRejectId callbacks are preserved (semantic)
new DriverLicense(IdCaptureRegion.Us) is present
ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins is preserved at startup (semantic)

evals.prompt: Just fix the scanner API in this file for the v8 upgrade — I'll deal with the rest later. Here's before-v7-idcapture.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill changes settings.scannerType = new FullDocumentScanner() into settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner(new FullDocumentScanner()).

assertions.text: new IdCaptureScanner( is present
new FullDocumentScanner( is present
settings.scannerType is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
.scanner = is present

evals.prompt: After upgrading to v8 I also want to reject expired documents and anyone under 18, using the new declarative settings rather than checking manually. Here's before-v7-idcapture.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill completes the v8 scanner/verifier migration and additionally sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true and settings.rejectHolderBelowAge = 18, noting these surface DocumentExpired and HolderUnderage in didRejectId.

assertions.text: settings.rejectExpiredIds is enabled
settings.rejectHolderBelowAge is present
new IdCaptureScanner( is present
these flags are described as surfacing reasons in didRejectId, e.g. DocumentExpired and HolderUnderage (semantic)
settings.scannerType is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)

evals.prompt: This ID Capture code is from an old v6 integration and no longer compiles on the current SDK (v8). Migrate it. Here's before-v6-idcapture.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill replaces the v6 supportedDocuments / IdDocumentType bitmask with acceptedDocuments populated by typed document objects (IdCard, DriverLicense, Passport with an IdCaptureRegion), replaces supportedSides with settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner(new FullDocumentScanner()), renames the onIdCaptured / onIdCapturedTimedOut listener to didCaptureId / didRejectId (timeout now surfaces as RejectionReason.Timeout in didRejectId), and reads the document type via capturedId.document?.documentType instead of capturedId.documentType.

assertions.text: settings.acceptedDocuments is populated with typed document objects, e.g. new IdCard(IdCaptureRegion.Any) (semantic)
new FullDocumentScanner( is present
settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner( is present
settings.supportedDocuments is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
IdDocumentType is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
settings.supportedSides is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
didCaptureId is present
didRejectId is present
onIdCaptured is no longer used as the success callback name in the migrated code (semantic)
onIdCapturedTimedOut is no longer used in the migrated code; timeout is handled via RejectionReason.Timeout in didRejectId (semantic)
RejectionReason.Timeout is present
the document type is read via capturedId.document?.documentType, not capturedId.documentType (semantic)
capturedId.documentType is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
